title: Alphabet locked in $811 billion of future spending on AI infrastructure. The number jumped $500 billion in one quarter.
excerpt: Alphabet had $811 billion in contracted future spending commitments at the end of June, up nearly $500 billion from just three months earlier. The figure, disclosed separately from capital expenditure in a quarterly filing, represents purchases Google has already committed to under supply agreements and open purchase orders. Roughly $200.7 billion of the total is […]
content_html:
TL;DR

Alphabet’s contracted future spending hit $811B at end of Q2, up ~$500B from Q1. It covers AI infrastructure supply agreements. Capex guidance raised to $195-$205B for 2026.

Alphabet had $811 billion in contracted future spending commitments at the end of June, up nearly $500 billion from just three months earlier. The figure, disclosed separately from capital expenditure in a quarterly filing, represents purchases Google has already committed to under supply agreements and open purchase orders. Roughly $200.7 billion of the total is short-term. The commitments cover chips, data centres, electricity, inventory, and content licences.

The number dwarfs the capex figures that have dominated AI spending headlines. Alphabet said on Wednesday it expects to spend $195 billion to $205 billion on capital expenditures in 2026 alone, up from its previous guidance of $180 to $190 billion. But $811 billion in total contracted obligations means Google has locked in roughly four years of spending at the current annual rate. Google’s AI search overhaul replaced links with AI-generated summaries, and the infrastructure behind that transformation is now the largest committed spending programme in the technology industry.

The $500 billion increase in a single quarter reflects how quickly the AI infrastructure race is accelerating. Microsoft, Meta, and Amazon are all expanding AI compute capacity at unprecedented rates, but none has disclosed a comparable figure for total contracted obligations. The commitments include long-term chip supply agreements, data centre construction contracts, and power purchase agreements that bind Google to spending years into the future regardless of how AI demand evolves.

Google is in talks with Marvell Technology to build new AI inference chips alongside its Broadcom TPU programme, and the $811 billion figure includes the supply agreements that underpin those hardware partnerships. Cloud revenue hit $24.8 billion in Q2, up 82% year-on-year, providing the revenue engine to service the commitments. But $811 billion is not a budget. It is a bet, locked in by contract, that AI compute demand will not slow down before the bills come due.

content_html_zh:
TL;DR

Alphabet 的未来合同支出在第二季度末达到 $811B，比第一季度增加约 $500B。 它涵盖人工智能基础设施供应协议。 2026 年资本支出指导上调至 $195-$205B。

截至 6 月底，Alphabet 的合同未来支出承诺为 8110 亿美元，比三个月前增加了近 5000 亿美元。 该数字与季度报告中的资本支出分开披露，代表谷歌已经根据供应协议和开放采购订单承诺的采购。 其中大约 2007 亿美元是短期资金。 这些承诺涵盖芯片、数据中心、电力、库存和内容许可。

这个数字让占据人工智能支出头条新闻的资本支出数字相形见绌。 Alphabet 周三表示，预计仅 2026 年资本支出就将达到 1,950 亿至 2,050 亿美元，高于之前 180 至 1,900 亿美元的指导。 但 8110 亿美元的合同义务总额意味着，按照目前的年增长率，谷歌已经锁定了大约四年的支出。 谷歌的人工智能搜索改革用人工智能生成的摘要取代了链接，而这一转变背后的基础设施现在是科技行业最大的承诺支出计划。

单季度 5000 亿美元的增长反映了人工智能基础设施竞赛加速的速度。 微软、Meta 和亚马逊都在以前所未有的速度扩展人工智能计算能力，但没有一家披露可比较的合同义务总额数据。 这些承诺包括长期芯片供应协议、数据中心建设合同和电力采购协议，这些协议将谷歌约束到未来数年，无论人工智能需求如何发展。

谷歌正在与 Marvell Technology 进行谈判，以与其 Broadcom TPU 计划一起构建新的 AI 推理芯片，8110 亿美元的数字包括支撑这些硬件合作伙伴关系的供应协议。 第二季度云收入达到 248 亿美元，同比增长 82%，为兑现承诺提供了收入引擎。 但 8110 亿美元并不是预算。 这是一个由合同锁定的赌注，即人工智能计算需求在账单到期之前不会放缓。
